questions: Should _check_worker_task_failure_streak (queries DB for failure history) also be absorbed into derive_worker_state(), or should it remain a side-effect in the health phase? Moving it would require making derive_worker_state() async (it's currently a pure function), which changes its testing story significantly.

The _perform_basic_health_check function (control_loop.py ~line 550) does a RunPod SSH check beyond just heartbeat age. Should this be kept as a separate real-time probe, or should its result be cached/folded into derived state? It seems like a genuine runtime check vs. a derivable verdict.

For Phase 2 (mixin wiring): the phases/ files currently contain copies of the old logic including the duplicate health checks. Should we update them in-place during Step 5, or first land Phase 1 on control_loop.py, verify it works, THEN update the mixin copies to match? (I assume the latter — incremental is safer.)
Is there a CI pipeline or specific test command to run? I see tests/ but want to confirm the right invocation (pytest, specific markers, etc).
success_criteria: DerivedWorkerState is the ONLY place that computes should_terminate verdicts — grep for heartbeat datetime arithmetic in control_loop.py returns zero matches outside of worker_state.py
_health_check_active_workers contains no if-branches that compute health verdicts — it only reads ws.should_terminate and ws.termination_reason and acts on them
_failsafe_stale_worker_check operates on List[DerivedWorkerState] not List[Dict] — no raw heartbeat parsing
startup_phase is checked in exactly ONE place: _determine_lifecycle in worker_state.py
All existing tests pass after Phase 1
control_loop.py reduced to <400 lines after Phase 2 (from 1699)
New unit tests cover: idle-with-queued-tasks termination, failsafe-stale derivation, startup_phase gating of failsafe
assumptions: derive_worker_state() should remain a pure (sync) function — we won't fold async DB queries like failure streak into it. Those checks stay in the health phase as action triggers, not verdicts.
The _perform_basic_health_check SSH probe is a runtime check that can't be pre-derived. It stays as a separate call but is only used as a supplementary signal, not a termination verdict source.
Phase 1 lands on the inline control_loop.py methods first. Phase 2 updates the mixin copies to match and wires them in. We don't try to do both simultaneously.
The control/phases/ mixin files are the target destination but their current contents are stale copies — they need to be rewritten with the Phase 1 logic, not merged.
Task failure streak checking remains a side-effect in the health phase for now, with a future option to cache results in derived state if needed.
The idle-timeout-with-no-work logic (control_loop.py:564-573) is already represented by is_not_claiming in DerivedWorkerState and doesn't need a separate field.
